steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The blank connects two statements about Ali's poem: (1) it "suggests the fluid interplay of poetry and painting," and (2) it "highlights fundamental tensions between these modes of representation." The transition must signal that these two ideas coexist simultaneously — the poem does both things at once, not one because of the other.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'At the same time,': Signals that the second idea exists alongside the first without contradicting it — the poem reveals both fluid interplay and fundamental tensions simultaneously. This is the correct contrast-and-coexistence relationship. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Therefore,': Signals a logical result, implying the tensions follow as a consequence of the interplay. That misrepresents the relationship — the two observations are independent, not cause and effect. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Specifically,': Signals that what follows is a detail or example of what came before, implying the tensions are a subset of the interplay. That is wrong — tensions and interplay are distinct aspects. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In other words,': Signals a restatement, implying both sentences say the same thing in different words. They do not — interplay and tensions are contrasting ideas, not equivalents. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
